top_signals.title: Accredited official statistics: HM Treasury: 2026 COVID-19 Cost Tracker update
top_signals.summary: Official Statistics on the cost of measures announced in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and how much the government has spent on these measures so far.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Billionaire Hotelier Wants to Take Japan’s APA Global
top_signals.summary: The family-run APA hotel chain that turned efficiency into a hospitality empire is betting it can export its uniquely Japanese formula abroad, starting with North America. APA Group CEO Isshi Motoya spoke with Bloomberg News' Momoka Yokoyama in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Piero Cipollone: Money in the digital age
SpaceX’s IPO Forces Wall Street to Reorganize Around It
top_signals.summary: The company’s ambitious listing plan is set to clear the way for other mega-offerings. It also risks threatening the integrity of the market itself

top_signals.title: The end of cheap
top_signals.summary: After 50 years of falling capital, labour and energy prices, the next half-century will look very different for America

top_signals.title: 'Gyaru' and jazz: Tourists get lost in Osaka's unexpected charms
Student loan borrowing is 'high stakes' as new rules take effect on July 1, CFP says. What to know
top_signals.summary: Experts say that student loan borrowers should proceed with caution when taking on more debt or consolidating after July 1, due to upcoming changes.

top_signals.title: Most planned natural gas pipeline capacity additions in 2026 and 2027 originate in Texas
top_signals.summary: Developers plan to bring approximately 44.9 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) of new pipeline capacity online in the United States in 2026 and 2027, according to our latest Natural Gas Pipeline Projects Tracker. Approximately 70% (31.6 Bcf/d) of this new capacity is already under construction. More than 66% (29.7 Bcf/d) of the capacity additions originate in Texas. Louisiana is second with 19% (8.4 Bcf/d) of total capacity additions.
